danger_signals.visual_form: Projectiles from stormtroopers and hostile droids
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring evasion or counterattack
Active Imperial patrol or defensive position

danger_signals.visual_form: Robed scavenger figures in desert environment
danger_signals.meaning: Territorial threat in early game / sandcrawler danger
High-level Imperial threat requiring evasion or piloting skill

danger_signals.visual_form: Industrial gray metallic corridors and technical structures
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy territory at highest danger level

safety_signals.visual_form: Glowing blade weapon drop from Obi-Wan Kenobi
safety_signals.meaning: Power-up enabling stronger offense and protection

safety_signals.visual_form: Various blaster modifications scattered through levels
Luke Skywalker, Han Solo, Chewbacca with distinct silhouettes
safety_signals.meaning: Different ability sets allow tactical safety through character selection

safety_signals.visual_form: X-wing cockpit or landspeeder perspective shifts
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary removal from ground-level threats via mechanical advantage

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Imperial insignia on uniforms and Death Star architecture
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Stormtroopers (white armor), TIE Fighters (gray twin pods), Darth Vader (black cape/mask)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Industrial Death Star interiors, gray metallic corridors, technical scaffolding, Imperial hangar bays

faction_indicators.color_identity: Light tones, Luke's blue/white tunic, Obi-Wan's tan robes
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Lightsaber as Jedi symbol, X-wing Rebel starfighter
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tatooine desert (sand-colored), Jedi hideouts, X-wing hangars, trench run finale

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Jawas as initial boss encounter in sandcrawler level
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tatooine desert, sandcrawler interior with conveyor belts, sand dunes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Desert environment with sand-colored architecture, Jawa presence, moisture vaporators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Luke begins as isolated hero; must fight through Jawa defenses to acquire droids

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shift from desert to spacecraft interiors; introduction of stormtroopers and technical environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player controls multiple heroes (Luke, Han, Chewbacca) indicating expanding resistance effort

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Late game - Direct assault on Death Star
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gray metallic industrial corridors, Imperial insignia prominent, TIE fighters visible through windows
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player transitions to X-wing pilot for trench run; Darth Vader confrontation signals Empire's direct response

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Camera perspective shift to cockpit view; X-wing or landspeeder replaces character sprite
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Movement and combat mechanics change; player gains mobility advantage over grounded enemies

environmental_storytelling.example: Tatooine's organic, sand-integrated structures versus Death Star's geometric industrial design communicates technological dominance of Empire
Game moves from isolated desert (Luke's origins) → populated spaces (infiltration) → enemy heartland (Death Star), mirroring film's escalation
Lightsaber drop from Obi-Wan marks transition from farm boy to Jedi, visually changing Luke's combat silhouette
Landspeeder and X-wing sequences break platformer monotony while representing Rebellion's technological capability

environmental_storytelling.technique: Boss character appearance as thematic emphasis
environmental_storytelling.example: Darth Vader's TIE Advanced x1 in final sequence visualizes his status as Empire's ultimate weapon and Luke's destiny

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fire blasters in coordinated bursts; patrol predictable paths until player is detected
Swarm player at sandcrawler entrance; act as mini-boss encounter requiring precise platforming
Chase player in vehicle sequences; indicate increasing Imperial response intensity
Appears with lightsaber after Death Star trench run; serves as climactic boss with unique combat patterns
Drops lightsaber power-up; appears as scripted encounter signaling narrative progression point
Visual distinction in movement speed and combat style; Han/Chewie availability indicates expanding mission team

notes: Super Star Wars uses film-accurate visual design to communicate faction identity and world state. The Empire is visually dominant through architecture (Death Star's industrial aesthetic) and enemy prevalence (stormtroopers, TIE fighters). The Rebellion is represented through player empowerment (lightsaber, vehicle control) rather than environmental design. Level progression mirrors narrative escalation: isolated desert → populated spaces → enemy stronghold. Vehicle sequences serve dual purpose: gameplay variety and strategic storytelling (player gains temporary technological advantage). No explicit political messaging beyond good-vs-evil Jedi/Empire dichotomy; world state communicates entirely through enemy placement, environmental design shifts, and cutscene narrative between levels.
sources_used: tcrf - Super Star Wars (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
